cover: Split-screen comparison infographic: 'Salesforce GraphQL API GA vs REST in 2026'. Deep navy blue bold header. LEFT COLUMN 'REST + SOQL Fan-Out' soft red/grey with blocks for 'GET /sobjects/Account', 'GET /query?q=Contacts', 'GET /query?q=Opportunities', 'GET /query?q=Cases' each with API call icon. Central flow diagram showing tangled lines between Mobile App, LWC Console, Apex Proxy, REST Resources. RIGHT COLUMN 'GraphQL API v64.0' soft green/blue with blocks for 'One typed query', 'Nested relationships', 'Client-shaped payload'. Central glowing node 'GraphQL /services/data/v64.0/graphql' with clean straight lines to Account, Contacts, Opportunities, Cases. Speedometer labeled FAST pointing green. Include small code excerpt: query AccountWorkspace { uiapi { query { Account { edges { node { Name { value } } } } } } }. Clean vector SaaS aesthetic, deep navy/teal/blue palette on light blue-grey background.
images: Dark blueprint developer infographic: 'REST Fan-Out vs Salesforce GraphQL API v64.0'. Deep dark navy background, glowing neon blue header 'REST Fan-Out vs Salesforce GraphQL API v64.0'. Subtitle 'One Account workspace should not require four network round trips'. TOP ROW: 3 horizontal cards — 'Latency compounds with each fetch with stopwatch icon', 'SOQL strings duplicate across clients with document icon', 'Partial failures multiply with warning triangle icon'. MIDDLE: side-by-side — LEFT red-themed box 'REST FAN-OUT' with red DANGER pill, RIGHT green-themed box 'GRAPHQL SHAPED READ' with green SUCCESS pill. CENTER: left column red border '// BAD' code: const account = await get('/sobjects/Account/001...'); const contacts = await query('SELECT Id, Name FROM Contact WHERE AccountId = ...'); const opps = await query('SELECT Id, Name FROM Opportunity WHERE AccountId = ...'); right column green border '// GOOD' code: query AccountWorkspace($id: ID!) { uiapi { query { Account(where: { Id: { eq: $id } }) { edges { node { Name { value } Contacts(first: 5) { edges { node { Name { value } } } } } } } } } }. BOTTOM: circular avatar 'Bennie Joseph | Architect' + checklist 'Reduce round trips', 'Shape payload at source', 'Keep REST for transactions'. Footer bar: 'Use GraphQL for read models, not every integration'. Monospace font for code, razor-sharp neon text, grid lines.

Dark blueprint developer infographic: 'GraphQL Does Not Replace SOQL'. Deep dark navy background, glowing neon blue header 'GraphQL Does Not Replace SOQL'. Subtitle 'Different layers, different jobs'. TOP ROW: 3 horizontal cards — 'GraphQL shapes API payloads with branching graph icon', 'SOQL powers Apex business logic with database icon', 'Apex owns transactions with shield icon'. MIDDLE: side-by-side — LEFT red-themed box 'WRONG COMPARISON' with red DANGER pill, RIGHT green-themed box 'RIGHT BOUNDARY' with green SUCCESS pill. CENTER: left column red border '// BAD' code: // Replace every Apex selector with GraphQL from the browser; fetch('/graphql', { body: hugeAccountQuery }); right column green border '// GOOD' code: Account a = [SELECT Id, Name FROM Account WHERE Id = :accountId WITH USER_MODE LIMIT 1]; Database.update(records, AccessLevel.USER_MODE);. BOTTOM: circular avatar 'Bennie Joseph | Architect' + checklist 'Use GraphQL for client-shaped reads', 'Use SOQL inside governed services', 'Use REST for stable operations'. Footer bar: 'Architecture is choosing boundaries, not chasing syntax'. Monospace font for code, razor-sharp neon text, grid lines.
